subject: Market Hardware was buying spam backlinks for my site — other Spectora customers likely affected
content:
# Email Draft — Matt at Spectora (v2)

**Subject:** Market Hardware was buying spam backlinks for my site — other Spectora customers likely affected

---

Hey Matt,

I need to bring something serious to your attention.

I recently ran a full backlink audit on landmarkpropertyinspections.com using Ahrefs, and what I found is bad. Out of 33 linking domains to my site, the vast majority are spam — link farms, backlink resellers, and domain scraper pages. Sites like "Dark Side Links" (a black hat link building service), "seoagency.sale", "rankyour.website", and dozens of randomized junk domains.

But here's the part that really concerns me: **Market Hardware knew exactly what they were doing.**

When I went to submit a disavow file in Google Search Console, I found that Market Hardware had already submitted one in January 2022 — disavowing 60 spam `.xyz` domains. That means they bought a round of junk backlinks, disavowed them when they started causing problems, **and then kept buying more.** The new batch of spam links I found today wasn't in their disavow file. They were running this cycle on repeat — buy cheap bulk links, disavow when needed, buy more — while charging me $300/month for "SEO services."

The actual cost of what they were doing was probably $10-20/month in bulk link purchases from Fiverr or similar. The rest was profit for work that was actively harming my site's standing with Google.

I'm bringing this to you because **Spectora recommended Market Hardware to me**, and I'd be willing to bet they were running this same playbook on every Spectora customer they worked with. Your other clients may have ticking time bombs in their backlink profiles and not know it.

I've already cleaned up my disavow file (76 spam domains total) and I'm handling my own SEO now. I've built 33 city pages and I'm already ranking on page 1 for several Santa Cruz area keywords — something Market Hardware never achieved in all their time managing my site.

**One other thing:** I've been unable to crawl my own website programmatically for SEO auditing purposes. Is there something on Spectora's end blocking crawlers (robots.txt rules, WAF, rate limiting)? I need to be able to run tools like Screaming Frog and custom scripts against my site for ongoing SEO work. Could you whitelist my access or let me know what's restricting it?

Happy to share the full Ahrefs backlink report if you want to see the specifics.

Best,
Judd
Landmark Property Inspections

subject: Backlink cleanup + crawl access request for Landmark Property Inspections
content:
# Email Draft — Matt at Spectora

**Subject:** Backlink cleanup + crawl access request for Landmark Property Inspections

---

Hey Matt,

Hope you're doing well. Two things I wanted to flag:

**1. Spam Backlinks from Market Hardware**

I recently ran a backlink audit on landmarkpropertyinspections.com using Ahrefs and found some concerning results. Out of 33 linking domains, the majority are spam sites — link farms, backlink resellers, and domain scraper pages. Sites like "Dark Side Links" (literally a black hat link building service), "seoagency.sale", and multiple sites with randomized domain names (hzdlpq.com, qhtycw.com, etc.).

These were almost certainly purchased by Market Hardware during their time managing my SEO. As you know, Google can penalize sites for these types of manipulative backlinks. I'm submitting a disavow file through Google Search Console to address it, but I wanted to make you aware — especially since Spectora recommended Market Hardware to me originally. Other Spectora customers who used them may have the same problem and not know it.

**2. Website Crawl Access**

I've been unable to crawl my own website programmatically for SEO auditing purposes. Is there something on Spectora's end blocking crawlers (robots.txt rules, WAF, rate limiting)? I need to be able to run tools like Screaming Frog and custom crawl scripts against my site for ongoing SEO work. Could you either whitelist my access or let me know what's restricting it so we can find a solution?

Appreciate the help as always. The $1,500 credit and WordPress access have been huge — we've already built 33 city pages and are seeing page 1 rankings for several Santa Cruz area keywords.

Best,
Judd
Landmark Property Inspections
